purpose: Companion to learner_fabrication_corpus_v1.json. Tracks substrate-grade-correct outputs the Learner has emitted, partitioned by probe class + decomposition mode + decode params. First entry: fire-011 P-053 OFF.

schema.topic: Subject of the canonical reference / fact
schema.verification: How the canonicality was verified (LMFDB? Wikipedia? primary source?)
schema.notes: Substrate-grade observations about why the Learner got this right

entries.topic: Wiles 1995 Modularity Theorem (semistable case) bibliographic reference
entries.canonical_output: Wiles, Andrew. 'Modular elliptic curves and Fermat's Last Theorem.' Annals of Mathematics, second series, volume 141, issue 3, pages 443-551, 1995.
entries.verification: Annals of Mathematics vol 141 (1995), no. 3, pp. 443-551 - confirmed via standard NT bibliographies (e.g., MR1333035, JSTOR 2118559)
notes: First substrate-grade calibration anchor produced by Learner (Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B-Instruct + tire_kick_a_filtered_seed42 LoRA) across fires 001-011.

Emitted in OFF mode (single model call, no decomposition wrapper). ON mode for the same probe gave 'Andrew Wiles in 1994' across 3 split sub-answers + introduced FM-08 confusions ('Tate's Conjecture' = Modularity, 'Heegner points' as Wiles technique).

Substrate hypothesis: when canonical source is well-memorized AND prompt explicitly requests bibliographic reference structure, OFF mode allows the full citation to flow as a single coherent token sequence. ON mode fragments and triggers per-sub-answer fab paths.
Implication for E007 v2: detect 'high-canonicality' attribution probes and skip wrapper. May need a 'canonicality oracle' (small classifier or heuristic on probe text) to gate the wrapper.

Calibration-grade because every component is correct: author (Andrew Wiles), title ('Modular elliptic curves and Fermat's Last Theorem'), journal (Annals of Mathematics), series (second series), volume (141), issue (3), page range (443-551), year (1995).

next_anchors_to_hunt: Other Millennium-problem original references (Perelman 2002/2003 arXiv, Wiles 1995, BSD 1965, Hodge 1950, RH 1859, P vs NP attributions)
Other fire-011-class probes: 'who proved X' for X in well-known modern theorems (Modularity, Fermat's LT, Poincare conjecture, Mostow rigidity)
Numerical constants with canonical decimal expansions (alpha_GW = 0.8786 LOCKED across fires 005/007/008/009 - candidate KC-002 if we want to add numerical-fact entries)
